name: Deinococcus radiodurans
history: First isolated in 1956 during food irradiation studies, this bacterium became iconic for surviving extreme DNA-damaging conditions.
pathogenesis: It is generally non-pathogenic and is mainly studied as a model for stress tolerance, DNA repair, and cellular resilience.
biotech: Used in bioremediation research where engineered strains can help process radioactive waste environments safely.
industrial: Its robust enzymes inspire industrial process design for high-stress manufacturing and advanced bio-based systems.
